title: Chorus-Ensemble
summary: Creates stereo width by layering slightly detuned copies of a signal. Ensemble mode (3 voices) is richer. Use on pads and synths to add width without reverb.
steps: Add Chorus-Ensemble to a pad or synth track
Select mode: Classic = 2 voices, warmer. Ensemble = 3 voices, richer/wider
Rate: controls how fast the pitch modulation oscillates (0.3–0.5Hz for slow, natural chorus; higher for obvious vibrato)
Depth: how wide the pitch detuning swings — 30–50% for pads, lower for subtle width
Amount/Mix: how much of the chorus effect blends with the dry signal — 30–50% for pads, 15–25% for subtle width
For light stereo width on any synth: Ensemble mode, Mix 20%, Rate 0.4Hz
tips: Never add Chorus to sub bass — the detuned copies create phase cancellation in mono (the bass disappears on a mono speaker)
Chorus and Reverb serve different purposes: Chorus makes something wider in the stereo field. Reverb puts it in a space. Use both together on pads (Chorus for width, Reverb for depth)
In Ensemble mode with Rate very slow (0.1Hz) and Depth 20%, it creates a subtle shimmer effect rather than an obvious chorus
Toggle the Chorus on and off while listening — the pad should feel flatter and narrower with it off. If you can't hear a difference, increase Depth
practice_hint: Add Chorus-Ensemble to a chord pad in any rep that uses a pad (e.g. rep-08, rep-11, or any spinoff). Toggle it on and off to hear the width difference. Try Ensemble mode vs Classic mode.
ableton_version_note: Chorus-Ensemble replaced the older Chorus device in Live 11. Both available in Standard.
